ocr.text: The dynamics of the North American tanker market are currently influenced by several key factors, including the status of the
newbuild order book and the prevailing spot market rates. As indicated in the accompanying table, the vessel type breakdown
reveals that bulk carriers are poised for significant growth, with a total TEU(000) of 3000.

This increase in capacity comes at a time when the financial metrics are under scrutiny; notably, the debt/equity ratio for bulk
carriers is currently at 0.45, suggesting a balanced financial structure, while the total vessels category remains unquantified with
a debt/equity of N/A.

Furthermore, the tanker segment has seen notable activity, with first-quarter delivery values reaching approximately $400M,
highlighting the ongoing investments in fleet expansion. On the spot market front, the MR rates are reported at 58.1, reflecting
current demand fluctuations. Additionally, the anticipated performance for the Panamax category in Q22023 is set to align with
TotalFY2023, while VLCC rates for the same quarter remain categorized as Q22023, underscoring the importance of timely
deliveries in a rapidly changing market environment. The Suezmax segment also shows promising indicators, with rates at
CAD/MT of 75.5, further emphasizing the robust nature of the tanker market amidst economic uncertainties.
